div.p: fd remains open. TCP connection survives. No SYN/FIN/RST. No kernel TCP state machine transition. No new file descriptor allocated. The OS three-way handshake cost (~1 RTT) was paid once for N requests.

p.strong: TCP connection lifespan covers both requests:

ul.li: Handshake RTT: paid once (~1ms LAN, ~50ms WAN)
Efficiency: saves 1 RTT + kernel TCP setup cost per subsequent request.
At 10K req/s with keep-alive: saves ~10K RTTs/s.

g.text: Keep-Alive State Reset (fd stays open — NO new TCP connection)
